shellCommands.command: /bin/bash -lc 'mempal search "downshift useMultipleSelection activeIndex Backspace Delete reducer selected item focus" --json --top-k 5'

/bin/bash -lc "git log --oneline --all -- src/hooks/useMultipleSelection/reducer.js | head -20; sed -n '180,245p' src/hooks/useMultipleSelection/index.js; sed -n '1,110p' src/hooks/useMultipleSelection/testUtils.js; cat package.json | sed -n '1,100p'"
/bin/bash -lc 'git show --stat --oneline HEAD; git show bf709b9 -- src/hooks/useMultipleSelection/reducer.js; git status --short'

/bin/bash -lc 'npx kcd-scripts test --no-watch src/hooks/useMultipleSelection/__tests__/getDropdownProps.test.js src/hooks/useMultipleSelection/__tests__/returnProps.test.js src/hooks/useMultipleSelection/__tests__/getSelectedItemProps.test.js && git diff --check && git diff --stat && git status --short'

/bin/bash -lc 'mkdir -p "$HOME/.mempal-notes" && cat > "$HOME/.mempal-notes/downshift-multiple-selection-delete-active-index.md" <<'"'EOF'
# Guard destructive reducer actions by active selection state

When a reducer removes an indexed item based on focus state, validate that the index is within the current collection before slicing. Sentinel values such as "'`-1` can make JavaScript slice expressions remove or duplicate unintended entries, so destructive key actions should return the existing state when no item is active.
EOF
mempal ingest "$HOME/.mempal-notes" --wing memory-evals'
